skill.name: fastapi-app
skill.description: Guides FastAPI backend development with app setup, routers, dependencies, Pydantic v2 schemas, async SQLAlchemy sessions, CORS, JWT authentication, rate limiting, and API documentation patterns.
skill.summary: Create FastAPI backend services with routers, validation, authentication, and database dependencies.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-severity signals, but most are false positives from Markdown code fences, sample file paths, and documentation links. The skill is not malicious, but it includes copyable FastAPI examples with weak JWT fallback secrets, placeholder database credentials, SQL echo logging, and broad CORS guidance that require a marketplace warning.

medium_findings.title: Weak JWT Secret Fallback in Generated Example
medium_findings.description: The authentication example reads JWT_SECRET_KEY but falls back to a literal placeholder secret. If copied into production, issued tokens could be forged by anyone who knows the sample value.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sample explicitly uses a static fallback secret for token signing. The issue is in generated guidance rather than executable marketplace code, so it is medium risk instead of a block.

medium_findings.title: Database Example Includes Placeholder Credentials and SQL Echo Logging
medium_findings.description: The database examples include a default connection string with user, password, host, and database values, and enable SQLAlchemy echo logging. Copied code can expose sensitive query data in logs and normalize hardcoded credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code sample clearly contains placeholder database credentials and echo=True. It is documentation, but users may copy it into real services.

medium_findings.title: Broad CORS and Host Binding Guidance Needs Production Guardrails
medium_findings.description: The examples show permissive CORS method and header settings, credentialed CORS, local origins, and uvicorn binding to all interfaces. These patterns are common for development but need explicit production restrictions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network-facing settings are visible and security-sensitive. They are not inherently malicious, but production misuse is plausible.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown Documentation
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick findings correspond to Markdown code fences, inline file names, and bash examples. No evidence found that the skill itself executes shell commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows Markdown fences and sample commands, not dynamic command execution logic. The bash block is user-facing setup guidance.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs are limited to local development origins, a placeholder production domain, and official project documentation links. No evidence found of data exfiltration endpoints or suspicious network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destinations are recognizable framework documentation or standard local development hosts. They do not receive secrets in the skill text.

low_findings.title: Environment File Access Is Standard Configuration Guidance
low_findings.description: The .env reference configures Pydantic settings loading and does not instruct the assistant to read or exfiltrate environment files. It should still be paired with secret handling guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a normal BaseSettings configuration example. There is no instruction to access existing user secrets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Secret Fallback Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Authentication code should require a strong JWT secret instead of defaulting to a known literal value.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fallback value is directly present in the sample and controls JWT signing. The concern is strong when the sample is copied.

dangerous_patterns.title: Verbose SQL Logging Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Database engines should not enable verbose SQL echo logging by default in production examples.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sample sets echo=True on the SQLAlchemy engine. This can expose sensitive query details in logs.

content.value_statement: Building FastAPI services requires consistent routing, validation, authentication, and database patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for production-oriented Python APIs.
actual_capabilities: Creates FastAPI application structure with lifespan setup and router registration.
Defines APIRouter endpoints with response models, pagination, and HTTP status codes.
Adds async SQLAlchemy session dependencies and database initialization patterns.
Builds JWT authentication dependencies with bearer token validation and role checks.
Creates Pydantic v2 schemas with field validation and ORM response compatibility.
Includes CORS, rate limiting, Swagger documentation, and implementation checklists.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a complete tested application by itself.
Security-sensitive defaults must be replaced before production use.
Examples target Python FastAPI backends and do not cover frontend implementation.
Database models and migrations still require project-specific design.

use_cases.title: Create a Backend API Skeleton
use_cases.description: Generate a FastAPI project layout with main app setup, routers, dependencies, schemas, and documentation notes.

use_cases.title: Add Authenticated CRUD Endpoints
use_cases.description: Build protected resource routes with role checks, pagination, validation, and consistent error responses.

use_cases.title: Standardize API Patterns for a Team
use_cases.description: Apply shared FastAPI conventions for CORS, JWT authentication, database sessions, and Pydantic v2 schemas.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic App
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI app with lifespan startup, Swagger docs, CORS for my frontend origin, and separate routers.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Resource Router
prompt_templates.prompt: Add authenticated CRUD routes for students with Pydantic v2 request and response models, pagination, and clear HTTP errors.

prompt_templates.title: Wire Database and Auth
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement async SQLAlchemy session dependencies and JWT bearer authentication for my FastAPI API. Require secrets from environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Harden an Existing API
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FastAPI backend for production readiness. Check CORS, JWT secrets, SQL logging, rate limits, validation, and error handling.

output_examples.input: Create a FastAPI backend for managing students.
output: A project layout with main app setup, routers, schemas, dependencies, and models.
Student list, detail, create, update, and delete endpoints with validation and role checks.
Notes for environment configuration, documentation, and test coverage.

output_examples.input: Add JWT authentication to my API.
output: Bearer token dependencies for current user and admin access.
Token creation and validation guidance with required environment secrets.
Error responses for invalid credentials and insufficient permissions.

output_examples.input: Make my FastAPI app ready for frontend integration.
output: CORS configuration guidance for known frontend origins.
Consistent response models and HTTP status behavior.
Swagger documentation and checklist items for API consumers.

best_practices: Require real secrets from environment variables and fail startup when they are missing.
Restrict CORS origins, methods, and headers to the deployed frontend needs.
Use async database sessions, response models, rate limits, and explicit error handling.
anti_patterns: Do not ship placeholder JWT secrets or database passwords.
Do not enable verbose SQL logging in production.
Do not expose administrative routes without role-based dependencies.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build FastAPI backend APIs with routers, dependencies, validation, authentication, CORS, and database patterns.

faq.question: Can it generate a full production service?
faq.answer: It can generate strong starting code, but project-specific security review, tests, migrations, and deployment work are still required.

faq.question: Does it support Pydantic v2?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance uses Pydantic v2 patterns such as ConfigDict, field validation, and ORM-compatible response models.

faq.question: Does it include authentication guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes JWT bearer authentication, current-user dependencies, and role-based access examples.

faq.question: Is it safe for production use?
faq.answer: Use it with review. Replace placeholder secrets, restrict CORS, disable SQL echo logging, and validate deployment settings.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
